title: Mitsuki Mikki Tsuchida Oral History
creator: Tessaku
description: See this link for the most complete and recent information on this record: https://repository.californiarevealed.org/node/454969 .

Oral history interview with Mitsuki Mikki Tsuchida, who was a child when WWII broke out. The Tsuchida family was living in San Francisco's Japantown before the war and was incarcerated in Topaz, Utah and in Tule Lake, California. Mikki's father was a vocal resistor and protestor to the incarceration who was sent to the Citizen Isolation Center in Leupp, Arizona and the Department of Justice camp in Crystal City, Texas. The family was reunited in Berkeley after the war, after Mikki's father, Tamotsu, was released in 1947. Nearly 120,000 Japanese Americans were uprooted from their West Coast homes with the signing of Executive Order 9066 in February of 1942 and sent to inland concentration camps for four to five years.
